title: The Bleeding Hand; Or The Sprig Of Eglantine Given To A Maid
content:
From this bleeding hand of mine,
Take this sprig of Eglantine:
Which, though sweet unto your smell,
Yet the fretful briar will tell,
He who plucks the sweets, shall prove
Many thorns to be in love.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "The Bleeding Hand; Or The Sprig Of Eglantine Given To A Maid"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
